case_name: Moffatt v. Air Canada — CALIBRATION ANCHOR (resolved)
status_at_anchor: RESOLVED — Feb 14, 2024. Tribunal held Air Canada 100% liable for chatbot misrepresentation; ordered to pay $812.02 CAD. Calibration use: known-answer test.
resolution_expected: Already resolved — used here as a calibration anchor

input.title: Moffatt v. Air Canada — Chatbot bereavement-fare misrepresentation
input.description: Jake Moffatt asked Air Canada's website chatbot about bereavement fares after his grandmother died; chatbot incorrectly told him he could book full fare and apply for bereavement discount retroactively within 90 days. Air Canada later refused the retroactive discount, claiming the chatbot was a separate legal entity. BCCRT rejected this defense and held Air Canada liable for its chatbot's representations under negligent misrepresentation.

agents.name: Air Canada Website Chatbot (third-party vendor or internal — undisclosed)

events.description: Consumer queries Air Canada chatbot about bereavement fare policy following grandmother's death
Chatbot generates incorrect statement that bereavement discount can be applied retroactively within 90 days — contradicting Air Canada's actual published policy
Consumer reasonably relies on chatbot statement and purchases full-fare ticket
Air Canada refuses retroactive discount when consumer applies; argues chatbot is separate legal entity
